title: lithodora consobrina
intro: Lithodora consobrina, also known as Dyer's Blue, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to create a vibrant blue dye from its flowers, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lithodora consobrina include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Lithodora consobrina can be made by infusing the flowers in hot water or using them as a tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential.
